name: Kapahulu
desc: The corridor between Waikiki and Kaimuki — local plate lunch joints, shave ice, real neighborhood feel despite tourist proximity.
insider: Rainbow Drive-In has been slinging plate lunches since 1961 — the mixed plate with gravy is the move, not the tourist combos
Ono Seafood has the best shoyu ahi poke on the island — go before noon because they run out of the good cuts
Side streets off Kapahulu have free parking within walking distance of Waikiki — locals park here and walk over
Leonard's Bakery malasadas are best fresh and hot — the line moves fast, get the haupia (coconut) filled ones
Uncle Bo's Pupu Bar & Grill does massive portions of local-fusion food — the garlic ahi and fried rice is a knockout
Waiola Shave Ice is the real local pick over Matsumoto's — the azuki bean with ice cream combo is perfect
The Kapahulu corridor between the freeway overpass and Waikiki is one long strip of authentic local eats — no need for tourist traps
Parking enforcement is aggressive on Kapahulu Ave itself — always read the signs for time limits and street cleaning days
Tonkatsu Tamafuji is a tiny Japanese spot doing some of the best katsu on Oahu — expect a short wait
The neighborhood sits on the edge of Waikiki's tourist zone but retains genuine local character — rents are 30-40% less than one block into Waikiki
